items.title: How to invest when everything is moving too fast
items.summary: TechCrunch's StrictlyVC evening in Los Angeles late last week brought together two of the more straight-talking investors working in AI right now. They were as entertaining as they were illuminating.

items.title: Walmart-backed Flipkart expands quick-commerce push as Amazon ramps up in India
items.summary: Walmart-backed Flipkart has crossed 1,000 micro-fulfillment centers as Amazon accelerates its own quick-commerce push in India.

items.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
items.summary: The all-cash deal gives MoEngage access to technology that assigns AI agents to individual customers.

items.title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
items.summary: Superhuman, which also has an AI detection tool as part of Grammarly, has snapped up GPTZero.

items.title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
items.summary: Menlo has created a solid rep for itself as an AI investor, all based on one gutsy $750 million move in 2024.

items.title: Klue says hackers stole credential from 2022 that led to customer data breaches
items.summary: It's unclear why Klue had not revoked the credential after the limited pilot, which hackers then used to breach a system holding keys for accessing customers' data.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market
items.summary: The app would be independent of Meta's other social media offerings, although sources told the NYT that those social sites could direct users to engagement with the app.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
items.summary: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

items.title: Password manager maker LastPass says hackers stole customer support case data during Klue breach
items.summary: This is the second data breach to affect LastPass customers in recent years, after one of the password manager's tech partners was recently breached.

items.title: HaloBraid raises $7M from Seven Seven Six to end the six-hour hair salon appointment
items.summary: HaloBraid aims to help salons speed up braiding with its first device, slated to launch later this year, that acts as a braiding assistant for professional stylists.

items.title: Meta debuts new, cheaper smart glasses under its own brand
items.summary: The smart glasses are available in several countries starting today in a variety of color and lens combinations.

items.title: 4 days left to save up to $190 on TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026
items.summary: Four days left to save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 — the ultimate founder bootcamp — before Early Bird rates end on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register today.

items.title: Ribbie turns real-time baseball stats into arcade-like, pixel-art broadcasts
items.summary: Ribbie lets you follow along live with MLB games with a delightful, arcade-inspired interface.

items.title: Fika Jobs raises $4M to build a video-first hiring platform where AI agents interview candidates
items.summary: Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok.

items.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
items.summary: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

items.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
items.summary: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

items.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
items.summary: Whether the Autopilot system was truly active, overridden, or malfunctioning likely won't be resolved until investigators finish combing through the vehicle's data logs.

items.title: Shareholders sue Uber’s board over sexual assaults, other incidents
items.summary: The lawsuit, led by a Detroit pension fund, alleges Uber's board and management has cut too many compliance corners, resulting in thousands of lawsuits.

items.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
items.summary: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

items.title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
items.summary: Microsoft inked a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron, locking in decades of carbon emissions from a new natural gas power plant.
